channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI’s new Operator is a step into AI’s agentic future
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday a research preview of Operator, an AI agent that can browse the web and perform tasks for the user. Operator is powered by the Computer-Using Agent (CUA), an AI model that merges GPT-4o’s vision capabilities with reasoning capability.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Subaru security vulnerability exposed millions of cars to tracking risks
item.description: Two security researchers discovered a security vulnerability in Subaru’s Starlink-connected vehicles last year that gave them “unrestricted targeted access to all vehicles and customer accounts” across the U.S., Canada, and Japan, according to a Wired report.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Palo Alto Networks firewalls have UEFI flaws, Secure Boot bypasses
item.description: Researchers have discovered that next-generation firewalls from Palo Alto Networks contain years-old known vulnerabilities in their UEFI firmware — a finding that provides yet more evidence of a broader issue with specialized devices today.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Trump AI project reignites feud between Musk, Sam Altman
item.description: Tensions between technology leaders Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman were on full display this week after the Tesla CEO slammed the new artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure project, Stargate, which is being funded in part by Altman's company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia Conference Boosts San Jose Hotel Development
item.description: San Jose hotel developers aim to finish construction on time for Nvidia's GTC event, expected to bring in significant revenue and economic growth.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia vs Apple and the world: Apple may have just confirmed its ACDC superchip will use UALink tech
item.description: Nvidia vs Apple and the world: Apple may have just confirmed its ACDC superchip will use UALink tech.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: AMD explains why RX 9070 GPUs won’t arrive until March – and this has got me more excited for RDNA 4, I’ve got to admit
item.description: AMD looks to be really gunning to take down Nvidia’s RTX 5070, and heated competition in the mid-range GPU space is an exciting prospect.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Canon reveals the world's first 410MP sensor – with a staggering 24K resolution and virtually infinite cropping potential
item.description: Canon's new 410MP full-frame sensor smashes previous records, offering a 24K resolution for images up to 8fps.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Opinion: Carter: How Trump’s TikTok ban reprieve could actually work
item.description: Trump is likely to use prosecutorial discretion, a freedom allowing him to decide which laws to enforce, when, and how.
item.dc:creator: Stephen L. Carter, The Mercury News

item.title: UK watchdog targets Apple, Google mobile ecosystems with new digital market powers
item.description: Google’s Android and Apple’s iOS are facing fresh scrutiny from Britain’s competition watchdog, which unveiled investigations targeting the two tech giants’ mobile phone ecosystems under new powers to crack down on digital market abuses.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Netflix's Zero Day trailer shows Robert DeNiro as a troubled former US president and I'm already on the edge of my seat
item.description: Robert DeNiro's new political thriller for Netflix, Zero Day, has certainly got my attention after watching a new trailer.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Netflix in February 2025
item.description: Netflix's February 2025 list packs another wave of must-watch titles, as well as the occasional time-waster. Here's everything new on Netflix in February 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Google to tweak Pixel Watch emergency calls as it tries to fix a problem Apple has had for years
item.description: A new Pixel Watch feature should introduce a new emergency calling feature that will make accidental calls less likely.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple Faces Major Blow in Unequal-Pay Lawsuit
item.description: A female employee's discovery of a male colleague's tax form on an office printer has led to a massive unequal-pay lawsuit against Apple, with the company facing significant challenges in its bid to kill the legal action.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon Closes Quebec Warehouses Amid Labor Dispute
item.description: Amazon closes all seven warehouses in Quebec, eliminating 1,700 permanent full-time jobs and sparking a labor dispute.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Silicon Valley bigwigs bask in Trump limelight in major reversal at inauguration
item.description: Trump scraps Biden executive orders on technology and artificial intelligence, but has brought Silicon Valley leaders into his close orbit.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Software bill-of-materials docs eyed for Python packages
item.description: Software bill-of-materials (SBOM) documents would be used in Python packages as a means to improve their “measurability” and to address the problem of “phantom dependencies” in Python packages, under a Python Enhancement Proposal (PEP) now being floated at python.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Today’s AI models have a poor grasp of world history
item.description: Today’s AI models do a poor job of providing accurate information about world history, according to a new report from the Austrian research institute Complexity Science Hub (CSH).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do
item.description: Robin Carnahan, the out-going GSA administrator, said the federal footprint shrunk by 11 million square feet and saved $2 billion in 2024 alone. The post Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: List: Tech moguls at Trump's inauguration
item.description: The heads of five of the top eight largest companies (by market cap) were seen at Trump's inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese tech firm shares robot training secrets with the world
item.description: A pioneering artificial intelligence and robotics company from China has developed a repository of over 1 million robotic movements to provide robotic training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World
